title: Introduction to AI Skills & Agents
abstract: What an LLM actually is vs. an agentic tool: IDE-based coding agents, GitHub Copilot, Copilot CLI, Claude Code. How to extend a generic LLM with custom instructions, reusable prompts, custom agents, skills, and MCP servers. Tokens, context windows, sub-agent orchestration, parallelization tradeoffs, teaching agents with your own patterns (Jira tickets, presentation files), and security considerations including cross-model review.

title: Big Refactoring Story
abstract: Anyone who has been working on a long-running project has come across a big, old, legacy piece of code that was hard to maintain. Complaints are common; actual successful rewrites are rare. The first challenge is convincing the business to give you the time. But a big refactor brings even more significant challenges: how to make sure you won't start complaining again about that code in a few months, how to deliver in an acceptable amount of time, how to involve the whole team, how not to lose features hidden in the legacy code, and how not to drift the planned solution mid-flight. My team got the time and solved all of these. I'll share how — from the code and from the process side — and the tips we worked out along the way. Spoiler: the refactor went so smoothly it became more of a success story than a trial-and-error one.

title: Kotlin Flow Operators
abstract: Kotlin Flow can be both simple and complex at the same time. Operators are a great example: they do simple things, but depending on how we use them they change their effects, making their usage tricky. I'll guide you under the hood of their implementation. With that knowledge you'll be able to predict how they will behave — and to write your own operators that interoperate with the library ones.

title: Good Kotlin with Java
abstract: Kotlin and Java live happily in the same codebase — until they don't. Patterns and pitfalls when writing Kotlin that has to be consumed cleanly from Java, and vice versa.

title: Chromecast for Android Developers
abstract: How the Chromecast SDK works, and what it takes to integrate cast support into a real Android app — sender, receiver, media routing, and the gotchas that aren't in the docs.

title: USB API on Android
abstract: A practical walkthrough of Android's USB host and accessory APIs — connecting to external hardware, permissions, and dealing with vendor quirks.
